corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

All R-CNN variants strongly outperform the three DPM baselines (Table rows 8-10), including the two that use feature learning.

The combination of HOG and sketch tokens yields 2.5 mAP points over HOG alone, while HSC improves over HOG by 4 mAP points (when compared internally to their private DPM baselines-both use nonpublic implementations of DPM that underperform the open source version ).

The first result, 20.9%, is what R-CNN achieves using a CNN pre-trained on the ILSVRC2012 classification dataset (no fine-tuning) and given access to the small amount of training data in val 1 (recall that half of the classes in val 1 have between 15 and 55 examples).
Our method achieves similar performance (53.3% mAP) on VOC 2011/12 test.

Most results in this paper use the network architecture from Krizhevsky et al. .
However, we have found that the choice of architecture has a large effect on R-CNN detection performance.
Following the PASCAL VOC best practices , we validated all design decisions and hyperparameters on the VOC 2007 dataset (Section 3.2).
We ran R-CNN on the 200-class ILSVRC2013 detection dataset using the same system hyperparameters that we used for PASCAL VOC.
In Section 4, we give an overview of the ILSVRC2013 detection dataset and provide details about choices that we made when running R-CNN on it.
To understand which layers are critical for detection performance, we analyzed results on the VOC 2007 dataset for each of the CNN's last three layers.

In Section 2 we presented results on the ILSVRC2013 detection dataset.
Object detection performance, as measured on the canonical PASCAL VOC dataset, has plateaued in the last few years.

In this paper, we propose a simple and scalable detection algorithm that improves mean average precision (mAP) by more than 30% relative to the previous best result on VOC 2012-achieving a mAP of 53.3%.
